rationale: The current best already exploits late quant-aware tuning and FP16 tail rescue, while recent schedule-only variants have flattened. This pivots to a new idea category without spending more artifact bytes: all remaining 2D int8 tensors now use a sqrt-companded per-row quantizer, which allocates more effective codebook resolution near zero where most trained weights live, then inverts that curve in both late roundtrip training and final exact evaluation. I also removed the now-unused per-tensor qmeta bookkeeping so the artifact stays lean and slightly more compressible under the 16MB cap.
title: Sqrt-Companded Matrix Int8 Roundtrip
